text.title: Capture Corpus Before Touching Legacy
text.summary: Build the golden-master corpus from production traffic, fixtures, or a fuzzer; normalize nondeterminism; commit the JSONL before any rewrite begins.

section.title: Capture and normalize
section.p: The corpus is whatever lets the test reproduce the legacy's observed behaviour without invoking the legacy. For pure functions, wrap the entry point and record `(input, output)` pairs while replaying production logs, a fixture set, or a fuzzer. Normalize sources of nondeterminism (timestamps, UUIDs, random IDs, ORDER BY-less query results) before writing the pair, otherwise replay flickers and the gate becomes useless.

rule.statement: Before any agent touches the legacy module, capture at least 200 distinct `(input, expected_output)` pairs into a versioned JSONL corpus under `tests/golden/`. The capture script MUST normalize timestamps (frozen value), random IDs (deterministic seed or strip), and order-sensitive collections (sort by stable key). The corpus file MUST be committed and immutable; new pairs append, existing pairs MUST NOT be silently rewritten.
rule.rationale: Untested legacy has no other spec. Without a captured baseline, every agent rewrite is unverifiable. Without normalization, the replay test goes flaky and gets disabled — the worst possible outcome.

example.code:
# capture_corpus.py — wraps legacy.process and writes pairs import json, pathlib, freezegun, random from legacy import process
random.seed(42) OUT = pathlib.Path("tests/golden/corpus.jsonl") with freezegun.freeze_time("2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"), OUT.open("a") as f: for case in load_production_inputs(): out = process(**case["input"]) out = normalize(out) # sort keys, strip volatile IDs f.write(json.dumps( {"id": case["id"], "input": case["input"], "expected": out}, sort_keys=True) + "\n")
example.note: Frozen time + seeded RNG + normalized output produce a reproducible corpus.

example.code: # Capture without normalization or freezing out = process(**case["input"]) # contains datetime.utcnow() + uuid4() write({"input": case["input"], "expected": out})
example.note: Replay never matches because timestamps and UUIDs change every run.

antipattern.description: Editing existing rows in the corpus when the rewrite changes output.
antipattern.reason: The corpus is the spec. Silent edits convert "rewrite parity check" into "rewrite is whatever the agent produced today" — the original goal is lost.

section.reference: Capture wrapper with freeze + seed + normalize.
HN thread: golden-master discipline as the AI-rewrite anchor.
